{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Takasaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakafutago Kofun | native_name =中二子古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|14.3|N|139|11|41.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park} | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster]] {{nihongo|'''Nakafutago Kofun'''|中二子古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nishi-Ōmuro neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Maebashi]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 7]] [[en:Naka Futago Kofun]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Takasaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakafutago Kofun | native_name =中二子古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|14.3|N|139|11|41.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park} | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster]] {{nihongo|'''Nakafutago Kofun'''|中二子古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nishi-Ōmuro neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}} [[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Maebashi]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Naka Futago Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[en:Naka Futago Kofun]][[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]]

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Takasaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakafutago Kofun | native_name =中二子古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|14.3|N|139|11|41.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park} | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster]] {{nihongo|'''Nakafutago Kofun'''|中二子古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nishi-Ōmuro neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Ushirofutago Kofun]] → [[Ushiro Futago Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Takasaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakafutago Kofun | native_name =中二子古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|14.3|N|139|11|41.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Maebashi, Gunma]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster]] {{nihongo|'''Nakafutago Kofun'''|中二子古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nishi-Ōmuro neighborhood of what is now the city of [[Maebashi, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Mae Futago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushiro Futago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}}

bumping these so I can see the more important ones that have not been recently edited
Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Text replacement - "|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}" to "|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}"

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nakafutago Kofun]][[en:Nakafutago Kofun]][[ja:中二子古墳]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Takasaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakafutago Kofun | native_name =中二子古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|14.3|N|139|11|41.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster]] {{nihongo|'''Nakafutago Kofun'''|中二子古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nishi-Ōmuro neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Mae Futago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushiro Futago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:History of Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Maebashi]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
}}

Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakafutago Kofun]][[en:Nakafutago Kofun]][[ja:中二子古墳]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Takasaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakafutago Kofun | native_name =中二子古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|14.3|N|139|11|41.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster]] {{nihongo|'''Nakafutago Kofun'''|中二子古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nishi-Ōmuro neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushiro Futago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]

==External links== {{commons category-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushiro Futago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}}

Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushiro Futago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nakafutago Kofun]][[en:Nakafutago Kofun]][[ja:中二子古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Nakafutago Kofun]][[en:Nakafutago Kofun]][[ja:中二子古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nakafutago Kofun]][[en:Nakafutago Kofun]][[ja:中二子古墳]] {{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Takasaki, Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Nakafutago Kofun | native_name =中二子古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_type = Japan Gunma Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nakafutago Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|23|14.3|N|139|11|41.3|E|region:JP|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Maebashi, Gunma]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = early 6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (Park} | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|right|270px|Ōmuro Kofun Cluster]] {{nihongo|'''Nakafutago Kofun'''|中二子古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Nishi-Ōmuro neighborhood of what is now the city of [[:en:Maebashi, Gunma|Takasaki]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushiro Futago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]]. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1927.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/204923|title=中二子古墳 |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is estimated to have been built around beginning of the 6th century AD and is part of a group of at least six tumuli which were concentrated in the vicinity, forming the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster. Within the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster are also the [[Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] and the [[Ushiro Futago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]], which have a separate National Historic Site designations.
==Overview== The tumulus is located on a low hill at the southern foot of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi}}. It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Omuro Park}} *[https://www.pref.gunma.jp/contents/100156310.pdf Gunma Prefecture tourism site] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.maebashi-cvb.com/spot/1036 Maebashi City guide] {{in lang|ja}}

It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|WD=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}

It is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is the largest in the Ōmuro Kofun Cluster with a total length of 111 meters, with a posterior circular portion in two tiers and an anterior rectangular portion two tiers, and is orientated to 89 degrees northeast. Part of the mound is carved out of the ground, and only the upper tier was originally covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. Cylindrical, house-shaped and other types of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' were also [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in profusion, and it is estimated that originally over 3000 ''haniwa'' were in rows on the mounds. The tumulus is surrounded by a shield-shaped double moat with crossings on the north and west sides. Although excavated in March 1891, no [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] was discovered.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Total length: 111 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 79 meters wide, 2-tier ;Posterior circular portion: 66 meter diameter x 15 meters high, 2-tier
The surrounding area is now maintained as {{nihongo|Ōmuro Park|大室公園¥¥}} with several other ''kofun'' and reconstructions of Kofun-period structures forming an [[:en:Archaeological park|Archaeological park]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|qid=Q16248234|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Gunma)}}
